app.name: UNMAPPED
app.tagline: Open skills infrastructure for the AI age.

landing.hero: 600 million young people. Unrecognised skills. Invisible to the formal economy.
landing.subhero: UNMAPPED maps real skills to real opportunity, with real data, country by country.
landing.amaraStory: Amara is 22, lives outside Accra, and holds a secondary school certificate. She speaks three languages, has run a phone repair business since she was 17, and taught herself basic coding from YouTube. By any reasonable measure, she has skills. To the formal economy, she is unmapped. She is not the exception, she is the rule.
landing.sectionTitle: Three failures. One open infrastructure layer.
landing.feature1Body: Map informal experience to ESCO + ISCO codes that travel across borders. Portable JSON + PDF export.
landing.feature2Body: Real wage and growth signals on every match. Formal, self-employment, gig, training. AI risk calibrated for LMIC context.
landing.feature3Body: Sector growth, wage distribution, automation calibration. Snapshot CSV export for program officers.
landing.footer: Data sources: ESCO · ISCO-08 · ILOSTAT · World Bank WDI · Frey-Osborne · ILO Future of Work · Wittgenstein Centre. Built as open infrastructure: no country hardcoded.

profile.title: Tell us what you know how to do
profile.subtitle: Mapped to ESCO (EU Skills Taxonomy) and ISCO-08 (ILO). Your profile is portable across borders and sectors, and explainable to you, not just to an algorithm.
profile.step3Heading: What have you done in real life?
profile.step3Hint: Add anything you have actually done, paid or unpaid. The matcher reads each entry as evidence.
profile.step4Heading: What did you actually do in that work?
profile.step4Hint: Pick everything that fits. These task primitives feed the AI displacement risk model.
profile.step5Heading: In your own words: what do you do?
profile.step5Hint: A few sentences. The more specific, the better the match.
profile.step1Hint: This helps us calibrate skill levels and credential mapping for your country.
profile.step2Heading: What languages and concrete skills do you have?
profile.step2Hint: Type freely. Press Enter or comma to add a chip. Click suggestions to add quickly.
profile.fieldYears: Years of work experience (formal or informal)
profile.fieldStory: In your own words, what do you do?
profile.languagesPlaceholder: Type a language and press Enter...
profile.sampleLoadedBody: You can edit any field before submitting.
profile.draftTitle: What will be sent to the matcher
profile.clarifyHint: I need a little more to map you accurately.
profile.linkCopiedBody: Send it to yourself; opening the link on any device restores your profile.
profile.loadedFromLinkBody: Restored from the saved link. You can edit and re-share.
profile.emailModalHint: We will send you a single link to your portable profile.
profile.emailPrivacyNote: Your profile is encoded in the URL itself. Nothing about you is stored on our servers.
profile.emailSentBody: Check your inbox in a moment.
profile.emailFallbackBody: We could not send directly; opening your mail client pre-filled instead.
profile.fieldSelfLearning: Where did you learn outside school?
profile.noWorkEntriesYet: No entries yet. Add at least one for a richer match.
profile.fieldTasks: What did you actually do in that work?
profile.fieldConstraints: What is realistic for you right now?
profile.constraintTravel: Furthest you can travel for work (km)
profile.constraintCanStudy: Can take time to study or train
profile.fieldAspirations: What would you like to do more of? (optional)
profile.aspirationsPlaceholder: A direction you would explore if given the chance
profile.voiceUnsupported: Voice input is not supported in this browser. Try Chrome, Edge, or Safari.
profile.navigatorBannerBody: You are entering a profile on behalf of someone else (for example a youth participant). The flow is the same; export or share the JSON or PDF so they can keep their own copy.
profile.savedToAccountBody: You can return later to continue from this profile.

opportunities.subtitle: Honest matches grounded in ILOSTAT wages, World Bank WDI sector growth, and Frey-Osborne automation scores, calibrated for LMIC context. No aspirational fluff.
opportunities.skillsAdjacent: Adjacent skills that would strengthen fit
opportunities.pathwaysHint: Formal, self-employment, gig, training. Generated for {country}.
opportunities.noLiveJobs: No live listings surfaced (or Tavily key not configured).
opportunities.noProfileBody: Map your skills first. Your opportunities are generated against your profile.
opportunities.matchingBody: Computing fit scores against ISCO-08 occupations and your local labour market.
opportunities.noMatchesBody: Add more specifics to your profile and try again.
opportunities.resilienceSubtitle: Composite of skill diversity, AI durability, sector momentum, and adjacency to top matches
opportunities.resilienceAiDurabilityHint: Inverse of average LMIC-calibrated automation risk
opportunities.resilienceSectorMomentumHint: Average YoY employment growth across top matches
opportunities.resilienceAdjacencyHint: Coverage of skills your top matches require
opportunities.wittgensteinYouthSubtitle: Wittgenstein Centre SSP2 Medium, share of youth (15 to 29) by education level. Same signal as the policymaker dashboard.
opportunities.wittgensteinFoot: Source: Wittgenstein Centre Human Capital Data Explorer (SSP2 Medium). Share of population aged 15–29 in {country} by ISCED bucket.
opportunities.compositeTertiary2030Sub: Projected share of cohort with tertiary education
opportunities.compositeUpperSec2030Sub: Projected share with upper secondary or higher
opportunities.compositeEduSpendSub: UNESCO UIS via WB, percent of GDP

dashboard.subtitle: Aggregate signals from ILOSTAT, World Bank WDI, ILO Future of Work, and Frey-Osborne, calibrated for {context} context.
dashboard.calibrationSub: The Frey-Osborne baseline is OECD-calibrated. We multiply per country.
dashboard.aiLensSub: Frey-Osborne scores averaged per sector, then calibrated with the per-country LMIC multiplier.
dashboard.wittgensteinSubtitle: Wittgenstein Centre SSP2 Medium: share of population aged 15–29 by education level (ISCED buckets).
dashboard.wittgensteinFoot: Source: Wittgenstein Centre Human Capital Data Explorer (SSP2 Medium). Share of population aged 15–29 in {country} by ISCED bucket.
dashboard.compGroupDemandSub: Live vacancy proxies and sector employment trends.
dashboard.compGroupSkillsSub: Calibrated Frey-Osborne and ILO Future of Work indices.
dashboard.compGroupRegionalSub: Connectivity indicators (ITU and World Bank, where available).
dashboard.compGroupInequalitySub: Gender employment gap and informality share.

admin.title: Country-agnostic by construction
admin.subtitle: The brief requires that labour data, education taxonomy, language, automation calibration, and opportunity types are configurable without changing the codebase. This page is the audit trail.
admin.diffSub: Same codebase. Different configuration objects. No rebuild required.
admin.stepsTitle: Add a new country in 4 steps
admin.step1: Add snapshot files under apps/api/src/signals/data/<code>/ (wages.json, growth.json, credentials.json, calibration.json) from ILOSTAT, WDI, and local TVET sources, or ingest via Admin upload.
admin.step2: Register the country in apps/web/lib/data/countries.json and apps/api/src/country/data/countries.json (keep in sync), set snapshot true for full wage or growth tables, or run scripts/generate-countries.mjs for the baseline registry.
admin.step3: Add or extend apps/web/locales/<lang>.json if the country needs UI strings in a new language.
admin.step4: Deploy API plus web; matcher, calibration, dashboard, and exports read the new configuration without code changes.
admin.footer: UNMAPPED is open infrastructure. Fork it. Localise it. The codebase does not change, only the configuration does.
